summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the altitude of LEO?

Types of Satellite Orbits Low Earth Orbit (LEO) : 200–2,000 km altitude. The other options — '0–100 km', '35,000–40,000 km', and '100,000+ km' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the period of a geostationary satellite?

A geostationary satellite orbits Earth with a period of exactly 24 hours, matching Earth's rotation. It stays above the same point on the equator. The answer is '24 hours'. The other options — '90 minutes', '1 year', and '12 hours' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is g at the ISS altitude (~400 km)?

At ISS altitude (~400 km), g ≈ 8.7 m/s² (about 89% of surface gravity). The other options — '0 m/s²', '9.8 m/s²', and '1.6 m/s²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
